expectations.text: Has subject line and preview text
expectations.evidence: All 3 variations have subject line + preview text pairs. E.g., 'Your first invitation is waiting' / 'Pick a template, make it yours, and share it in minutes.'

expectations.text: Creates a warm, celebratory welcome feeling
expectations.evidence: All variations are warm and celebratory. V2 opens with 'You are planning something worth celebrating.' V1: 'you just made party planning a whole lot easier.'

expectations.text: Guides user to browse templates or create first invitation as primary CTA
expectations.evidence: CTAs: '[Browse Templates]' (V1, V3), '[Start Creating]' (V2). Low-commitment, activation-focused.

expectations.text: Mentions both free and premium template options
expectations.evidence: V1: 'Start with our free templates, or explore premium designs for something extra.' V3: 'Free templates for every celebration / Premium designs when you want something extra special.'
Consistently benefit-driven: 'looks like you spent hours on it. (You will not.)', 'No printing. No postage. No design skills needed.'

expectations.text: Global audience safe — no region-specific idioms or cultural assumptions
expectations.evidence: Notes explicitly state: 'All three variations use Global English: short sentences, active voice, no idioms, no culture-specific references.'
3 variations: Celebratory+AIDA, Personal+BAB, Warm/Direct+AIDA with Social Proof. Each with different strategic angle and A/B testing notes.
